2026-03-10.title: West Bengal SIR test: reading the Supreme Court’s order
2026-03-10.title_hi: पश्चिम बंगाल में चुनावी मतदाता सूची विवाद: सुप्रीम कोर्ट के आदेश की व्याख्या
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Election Commission and Electoral Reforms
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court's order comes in the context of 60 lakh cases pending adjudication in West Bengal. The publication of final rolls has sparked debate on the role of the Election Commission and the need for permanent staff. The Supreme Court's decision aims to expedite the resolution of electoral roll disputes. The Election Commission has been facing challenges in managing the large number of cases. The Supreme Court's order is expected to bring relief to the Election Commission and ensure the integrity of the electoral process.
key_points: The Supreme Court — Order: Deployment of judicial officers to decide electoral roll disputes in West Bengal.
Election Commission — Staffing: Debate on whether the Election Commission should be equipped with permanent staff.
West Bengal — Electoral Roll Disputes: 60 lakh cases pending adjudication.
The Supreme Court — Decision: Aims to expedite the resolution of electoral roll disputes.
Election Commission — Challenge: Managing the large number of electoral roll disputes.

2026-03-10.title: Prime Minister to launch projects worth ₹10,800 crore at Kochi event
2026-03-10.title_hi: प्रधान मंत्री ₹10,800 करोड़ की परियोजनाओं का कोच्चि में शिलान्यास करेंगे
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: The Prime Minister will lay the foundation stone for a polypropylene unit at the Kochi Refinery, which is part of the projects worth ₹10,800 crore. The event in Kochi will also see the inauguration of 23 rural roads constructed under the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana in the State. The Kochi Refinery is a major project that aims to boost the region's economy. The Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana is a government scheme that focuses on constructing rural roads to improve connectivity. The total cost of the projects is ₹10,800 crore, which will be invested in the region.
key_points: The Prime Minister — Kochi Refinery: Laying the foundation stone for a polypropylene unit.
The Prime Minister — Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana: Inaugurating 23 rural roads constructed under the scheme.
The Government — Projects: Launching projects worth ₹10,800 crore in Kochi.
The Kochi Refinery — Polypropylene Unit: Aiming to boost the region's economy with the new unit.
The Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana — Rural Roads: Focusing on constructing rural roads to improve connectivity.

2026-03-10.title: Supreme Court approves Centre's proposal extending term of tribunal members
2026-03-10.title_hi: सुप्रीम कोर्ट ने केंद्र के प्रस्ताव को मंजूरी दी, ट्रिब्यूनल सदस्यों का कार्यकाल बढ़ाया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Judiciary and Tribunal System
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court has approved the Centre's proposal to extend the term of tribunal members appointed under the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021. This decision will affect around 21 members who are due to retire by next September. The Attorney General, R Venkataramani, stated that a new law is likely to be enacted by the next Budget session or the Monsoon session. The Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021 was enacted to reform the tribunal system in India. The extension of the term of tribunal members will provide continuity and stability to the system.
key_points: Supreme Court — Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021: Approved the Centre's proposal to extend the term of tribunal members.
Attorney General — R Venkataramani: Stated that a new law is likely to be enacted by the next Budget session or the Monsoon session.
Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021 — Extension: Will affect around 21 members who are due to retire by next September.
Supreme Court — Tribunal System: Provided continuity and stability to the system by extending the term of tribunal members.
Centre — Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021: Proposed the extension of the term of tribunal members to ensure a smooth transition.

2026-03-10.title: We will have to approach court against Centre to seek justice on devolution of funds, says CM Siddaramaiah
2026-03-10.title_hi: केंद्र से न्याय पाने के लिए हमें फंड आवंटन पर अदालत का दरवाजा खटखटाना पड़ सकता है: सीएम सिद्धारमैया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Interstate Relations and Centre-State Relations
2026-03-10.context: The Karnataka government has been seeking more funds from the Centre. The state had earlier received drought relief assistance of about ₹3,000 crore from the Centre after approaching the Supreme Court. The Chief Minister's statement comes amid a long-standing dispute between the state and the Centre over fund allocation. The state government has been arguing that it has not received its fair share of funds. The Centre's decision on fund devolution has been a point of contention between the two.
key_points: The Karnataka government: may approach the court to seek justice on fund devolution from the Centre.
The Centre: had earlier provided ₹3,000 crore in drought relief assistance to Karnataka after a Supreme Court intervention.
The state government: has been arguing for a greater share of funds from the Centre.
The Chief Minister: stated that the state may have to take legal action to resolve the issue.
The Supreme Court: has previously ruled in favour of Karnataka in a similar case, providing ₹3,000 crore in relief.

2026-03-10.title: UP govt signs pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity
2026-03-10.title_hi: उत्तर प्रदेश सरकार ने हिंदुजा समूह की कंपनी के साथ ब्रॉडबैंड कनेक्टिविटी विस्तार के लिए समझौता किया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: The Uttar Pradesh government has signed a pact with a Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity in the state. The pact aims to provide high-speed internet services to around 3.5 lakh gram panchayats and 30,000 villages. The project is expected to be completed within the next 2 years at a cost of ₹2,500 crore. The Hinduja Group firm will be responsible for laying down the optical fibre network and providing the necessary infrastructure for the project.
key_points: Uttar Pradesh government — Broadband Expansion Project: Signed pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity.
Hinduja Group firm — Optical Fibre Network: Will lay down optical fibre network and provide necessary infrastructure for the project.
Uttar Pradesh government — Project Cost: Expected to be completed at a cost of ₹2,500 crore.
Uttar Pradesh government — Project Timeline: Expected to be completed within the next 2 years.
Uttar Pradesh government — Beneficiaries: Will provide high-speed internet services to around 3.5 lakh gram panchayats and 30,000 villages.
